questions.stem: According to the DSM-5-TR, the ten specific personality disorders are organized using which classification approach?
options: A categorical model grouping disorders into three clusters based on shared descriptive features
A dimensional model rating personality traits along five continuous spectra derived from the Big Five
A hierarchical model with a single general personality pathology factor at the top and specific disorders below
A prototype matching model requiring clinicians to rate overall similarity to ideal disorder descriptions
questions.answer: A categorical model grouping disorders into three clusters based on shared descriptive features
questions.explanation: The DSM-5-TR uses a categorical classification model in which the ten personality disorders are organized into three clusters based on shared descriptive characteristics: Cluster A (odd/eccentric: paranoid, schizoid, schizotypal), Cluster B (dramatic/emotional/erratic: antisocial, borderline, histrionic, narcissistic), and Cluster C (anxious/fearful: avoidant, dependent, obsessive-compulsive). Option B describes a dimensional approach (the alternative DSM-5 model in Section III, not the primary classification). Option C describes a hierarchical factor model that is a research framework but not the DSM-5-TR's primary organization. Option D describes prototype matching, which is an alternative diagnostic approach not used as the primary DSM-5-TR system.
questions.kn_explanation: This question tests knowledge of the DSM-5-TR's organizational framework for personality disorders, a core diagnostic classification concept.
questions.quality_comment: Foundational question about personality disorder classification. Clearly supported by lesson content describing the three-cluster categorical model.
questions.suggestedParagraph: ## The Three Clusters: Your Organizational Framework
questions.lessonExcerpt: The DSM-5-TR organizes the ten personality disorders into three clusters based on shared characteristics... Cluster A: Odd, eccentric behaviors... Cluster B: Dramatic, emotional, erratic behaviors... Cluster C: Anxious, fearful behaviors.
